Abstract: Data assimilation (DA) for systems governed by partial differential equations (PDE) aims to reconstruct full spatiotemporal fields from sparse high-fidelity (HF) observations while respecting physical constraints. While full-grid low-fidelity (LF) simulations provide informative priors in multi-fidelity settings, recovering an HF field consistent with both sparse observations and the governing PDE typically requires per-instance test-time optimization, which becomes a major bottleneck in time-critical applications. To alleviate this, amortized reconstruction using generative models has recently been proposed; however, such approaches rely on full-field HF supervision during training, which is often impractical in real-world settings. From a more realistic perspective, we propose the Physics-Informed Conditional Schr\"odinger Bridge (PICSB), which transports an informative LF prior toward an observation-conditioned HF posterior without any additional inference-time guidance. To enable learning without HF endpoints, PICSB employs an iterative surrogate-endpoint refresh scheme, and directly incorporates PDE residuals into the training objective while enforcing observations via hard conditioning throughout sampling. Experiments on fluid PDE benchmarks demonstrate that PICSB enables extremely fast spatiotemporal field reconstruction while maintaining competitive accuracy under sparse HF supervision.

zh.title: 稀疏监督数据同化通过物理信息薛定谔桥
zh.summary: PICSB框架通过对低保真先验进行条件化生成模型并纳入PDE约束，从稀疏观测中实现快速时空场重建，消除了训练期间对全场监督的需求。

fr.title: Assimilation de Données Faiblement Supervisées via le Pont de Schrödinger Informé par la Physique
fr.summary: Le cadre PICSB permet une reconstruction rapide de champs spatio-temporels à partir d'observations clairsemées en conditionnant les modèles génératifs sur des priors de basse fidélité tout en incorporant des contraintes EDP, éliminant le besoin de supervision du champ complet lors de l'entraînement.

de.title: Dünn Überwachte Datenassimilation durch Physik-informierte Schrödinger-Brücke
de.summary: Das PICSB-Framework ermöglicht schnelle raum-zeitliche Feldrekonstruktion aus spärlichen Beobachtungen durch Konditionierung generativer Modelle auf niedrig-fidelitätspriors bei Einbeziehung von PDE-Nebenbedingungen, was die Notwendigkeit vollständiger Feldüberwachung während des Trainings beseitigt.

es.title: Asimilación de Datos Débilmente Supervisada mediante Puente de Schrödinger Informado por Física
es.summary: El marco PICSB permite la reconstrucción rápida de campos espacio-temporales a partir de observaciones dispersas condicionando modelos generativos en priors de baja fidelidad mientras se incorporan restricciones de EDP, eliminando la necesidad de supervisión de campo completo durante el entrenamiento.

title: From Instructions to Assistance: a Dataset Aligning Instruction Manuals with Assembly Videos for Evaluating Multimodal LLMs
summary: M2AD dataset evaluates multimodal LLMs on furniture assembly tasks by aligning instruction manuals with video, revealing performance limitations in multi-image reasoning and architectural constraints for real-time technical assistance.
content:
arXiv:2603.22321v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The recent advancements introduced by Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can support complex, real world tasks, pushing research outside the text boundaries towards multi modal contexts and leading to Multimodal Large Language Models (MLMs). Given the current adoption of LLM based assistants in solving technical or domain specific problems, the natural continuation of this trend is to extend the input domains of these assistants exploiting MLMs. Ideally, these MLMs should be used as real time assistants in procedural tasks, hopefully integrating a view of the environment where the user being assisted is, or even better sharing the same point of view via Virtual Reality (VR) or Augmented Reality (AR) supports, to reason over the same scenario the user is experiencing. With this work, we aim at evaluating the quality of currently openly available MLMs to provide this kind of assistance on technical tasks. To this end, we annotated a data set of furniture assembly with step by step labels and manual references: the Manual to Action Dataset (M2AD). We used this dataset to assess (1) to which extent the reasoning abilities of MLMs can be used to reduce the need for detailed labelling, allowing for more efficient, cost effective annotation practices, (2) whether MLMs are able to track the progression of assembly steps (3) and whether MLMs can refer correctly to the instruction manual pages. Our results showed that while some models understand procedural sequences, their performance is limited by architectural and hardware constraints, highlighting the need for multi image and interleaved text image reasoning.

zh.title: 从指令到协助：对齐说明书与装配视频以评估多模态大语言模型的数据集
zh.summary: M2AD数据集通过将说明书与视频对齐来评估多模态大语言模型在家具组装任务上的能力，揭示了多图像推理的性能限制和实时技术协助的架构约束。

fr.title: Des Instructions à l'Assistance : un Ensemble de Données Alignant les Manuels d'Instructions avec des Vidéos d'Assemblage pour Évaluer les LLM Multimodaux
fr.summary: L'ensemble de données M2AD évalue les LLM multimodaux sur des tâches d'assemblage de meubles en alignant les manuels d'instructions avec la vidéo, révélant les limitations de performance dans le raisonnement multi-image et les contraintes architecturales pour l'assistance technique en temps réel.

de.title: Von Anweisungen zu Unterstützung: Ein Datensatz zur Ausrichtung von Bedienungsanleitungen mit Montagevideos zur Bewertung multimodaler LLMs
de.summary: Der M2AD-Datensatz bewertet multimodale LLMs bei Möbelmontageaufgaben durch Ausrichtung von Bedienungsanleitungen mit Video und zeigt Leistungsbeschränkungen bei Multi-Bild-Reasoning und architektonische Einschränkungen für echtzeitliche technische Unterstützung.

es.title: De Instrucciones a Asistencia: un Conjunto de Datos Alineando Manuales de Instrucciones con Videos de Montaje para Evaluar LLM Multimodales
es.summary: El conjunto de datos M2AD evalúa LLM multimodales en tareas de montaje de muebles alineando manuales de instrucciones con video, revelando limitaciones de rendimiento en razonamiento multi-imagen y restricciones arquitectónicas para asistencia técnica en tiempo real.

title: AEGIS: An Operational Infrastructure for Post-Market Governance of Adaptive Medical AI Under US and EU Regulations
summary: AEGIS governance framework operationalizes FDA and EU regulatory requirements for adaptive medical AI systems through automated monitoring, retraining, and deployment decisions, with demonstrated application to sepsis prediction and tumor segmentation.
content:
arXiv:2603.22322v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Machine learning systems deployed in medical devices require governance frameworks that ensure safety while enabling continuous improvement. Regulatory bodies including the FDA and European Union have introduced mechanisms such as the Predetermined Change Control Plan (PCCP) and Post-Market Surveillance (PMS) to manage iterative model updates without repeated submissions. This paper presents AI/ML Evaluation and Governance Infrastructure for Safety (AEGIS), a governance framework applicable to any healthcare AI system. AEGIS comprises three modules, i.e., dataset assimilation and retraining, model monitoring, and conditional decision, that operationalize FDA PCCP and EU AI Act Article 43(4) provisions. We implement a four-category deployment decision taxonomy (APPROVE, CONDITIONAL APPROVAL, CLINICAL REVIEW, REJECT) with an independent PMS ALARM signal, enabling detection of the critical state in which no deployable model exists while the released model is simultaneously at risk.
  To illustrate how AEGIS can be instantiated across heterogeneous clinical contexts, we provide two examples: sepsis prediction from electronic health records and brain tumor segmentation from medical imaging. Both cases use identical governance architecture, differing only in configuration. Across 11 simulated iterations on the sepsis example, AEGIS yielded 8 APPROVE, 1 CONDITIONAL APPROVAL, 1 CLINICAL REVIEW, and 1 REJECT decision, exercising all four categories. ALARM signals were co-issued at iterations 8 and 10, including the critical state where no deployable model exists and the released model is simultaneously failing. AEGIS detected drift before observable performance degradation. These results demonstrate that AEGIS translates regulatory change-control concepts into executable governance procedures, supporting safe continuous learning for adaptive medical AI across diverse clinical applications.

zh.title: AEGIS：美国和欧盟法规下自适应医疗AI的市场后治理运营基础设施
zh.summary: AEGIS治理框架通过自动化监测、再训练和部署决策来实现FDA和欧盟对自适应医疗AI系统的监管要求，并在脓毒症预测和肿瘤分割中展示了应用。

fr.title: AEGIS : Une Infrastructure Opérationnelle pour la Gouvernance Post-Marché de l'IA Médicale Adaptative en Vertu des Réglementations Américaines et Européennes
fr.summary: Le cadre de gouvernance AEGIS opérationnalise les exigences réglementaires de la FDA et de l'UE pour les systèmes d'IA médicale adaptative par le biais de la surveillance automatisée, du réentraînement et des décisions de déploiement, avec une application démontrée à la prédiction de la septicémie et à la segmentation des tumeurs.

de.title: AEGIS: Eine operative Infrastruktur für die Governance adaptiver medizinischer KI nach dem Markteintritt gemäß US- und EU-Vorschriften
de.summary: Das AEGIS-Governance-Framework operationalisiert FDA- und EU-Anforderungen für adaptive medizinische KI-Systeme durch automatisierte Überwachung, Umschulung und Bereitstellungsentscheidungen mit nachgewiesener Anwendung auf Sepsisvorhersage und Tumorsegmentierung.

es.title: AEGIS: Una Infraestructura Operativa para la Gobernanza Post-Mercado de IA Médica Adaptativa bajo Regulaciones de EE.UU. y la UE
es.summary: El marco de gobernanza AEGIS operacionaliza los requisitos regulatorios de la FDA y la UE para sistemas de IA médica adaptativa a través de monitoreo automatizado, reentrenamiento y decisiones de implementación, con aplicación demostrada a la predicción de sepsis y segmentación de tumores.

title: A Multi-Task Targeted Learning Framework for Lithium-Ion Battery State-of-Health and Remaining Useful Life
summary: Multi-task framework combining multi-scale CNNs, extended LSTM, and dual-stream attention substantially improves battery state-of-health and remaining useful life predictions, enhancing safety and efficiency of electric vehicle operations.
content:
arXiv:2603.22323v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Accurately predicting the state-of-health (SOH) and remaining useful life (RUL) of lithium-ion batteries is crucial for ensuring the safe and efficient operation of electric vehicles while minimizing associated risks. However, current deep learning methods are limited in their ability to selectively extract features and model time dependencies for these two parameters. Moreover, most existing methods rely on traditional recurrent neural networks, which have inherent shortcomings in long-term time-series modeling. To address these issues, this paper proposes a multi-task targeted learning framework for SOH and RUL prediction, which integrates multiple neural networks, including a multi-scale feature extraction module, an improved extended LSTM, and a dual-stream attention module. First, a feature extraction module with multi-scale CNNs is designed to capture detailed local battery decline patterns. Secondly, an improved extended LSTM network is employed to enhance the model's ability to retain long-term temporal information, thus improving temporal relationship modeling. Building on this, the dual-stream attention module-comprising polarized attention and sparse attention to selectively focus on key information relevant to SOH and RUL, respectively, by assigning higher weights to important features. Finally, a many-to-two mapping is achieved through the dual-task layer. To optimize the model's performance and reduce the need for manual hyperparameter tuning, the Hyperopt optimization algorithm is used. Extensive comparative experiments on battery aging datasets demonstrate that the proposed method reduces the average RMSE for SOH and RUL predictions by 111.3\% and 33.0\%, respectively, compared to traditional and state-of-the-art methods.

zh.title: 锂离子电池健康状态和剩余寿命的多任务目标学习框架
zh.summary: 多任务框架结合多尺度卷积神经网络、扩展长短期记忆和双流注意机制，大幅提高了电池健康状态和剩余寿命预测的准确性，增强了电动汽车运行的安全性和效率。

fr.title: Un cadre d'apprentissage ciblé multi-tâches pour l'état de santé et la durée de vie utile restante des batteries lithium-ion
fr.summary: Le cadre multi-tâches combinant des CNN multi-échelles, un LSTM étendu et une attention bidirectionnelle améliore considérablement les prédictions de l'état de santé et de la durée de vie utile restante des batteries, améliorant la sécurité et l'efficacité des opérations des véhicules électriques.

de.title: Ein Multi-Task-Ziellernen-Framework für den Gesundheitszustand und die verbleibende Lebensdauer von Lithium-Ionen-Batterien
de.summary: Das Multi-Task-Framework kombiniert Multi-Skalen-CNNs, erweitertes LSTM und duale Stream-Aufmerksamkeit und verbessert erheblich die Vorhersagen des Batterie-Gesundheitszustands und der verbleibenden Lebensdauer und erhöht die Sicherheit und Effizienz des Elektrofahrzeugbetriebs.

es.title: Un marco de aprendizaje dirigido multi-tarea para el estado de salud y la vida útil restante de las baterías de iones de litio
es.summary: El marco multi-tarea que combina CNN multiescala, LSTM extendido y atención de doble flujo mejora sustancialmente las predicciones del estado de salud y la vida útil restante de la batería, mejorando la seguridad y eficiencia de las operaciones de vehículos eléctricos.

title: DAQ: Delta-Aware Quantization for Post-Training LLM Weight Compression
summary: DAQ post-training quantization framework preserves post-training knowledge by optimizing for weight delta directional fidelity rather than reconstruction error, recovering style-specific LLM capabilities under FP8 compression.
content:
arXiv:2603.22324v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We introduce Delta-Aware Quantization (DAQ), a data-free post-training quantization framework that preserves the knowledge acquired during post-training. Standard quantization objectives minimize reconstruction error but are agnostic to the base model, allowing quantization noise to disproportionately corrupt the small-magnitude parameter deltas ($\Delta W$) that encode post-training behavior -- an effect we analyze through the lens of quantization as implicit regularization. DAQ replaces reconstruction-based objectives with two delta-aware metrics -- Sign Preservation Rate and Cosine Similarity -- that directly optimize for directional fidelity of $\Delta W$, requiring only the base and post-trained weight matrices. In a pilot FP8 study, DAQ recovers style-specific capabilities lost under standard quantization while maintaining general performance.

zh.title: DAQ：用于后训练LLM权重压缩的增量感知量化
zh.summary: DAQ后训练量化框架通过优化权重增量方向保真度而非重构误差来保持后训练知识，在FP8压缩下恢复风格特定的大型语言模型能力。

fr.title: DAQ : Quantification sensible aux deltas pour la compression des poids LLM post-entraînement
fr.summary: Le cadre de quantification post-entraînement DAQ préserve les connaissances post-entraînement en optimisant la fidélité directionnelle delta des poids plutôt que l'erreur de reconstruction, récupérant les capacités LLM spécifiques au style sous compression FP8.

de.title: DAQ: Delta-bewusste Quantisierung zur Gewichtskompression von LLM nach dem Training
de.summary: Das DAQ-Post-Training-Quantisierungsframework bewahrt post-Training-Wissen durch Optimierung der Richtungstreue des Gewichtdeltas statt des Rekonstruktionsfehlers und stellt LLM-Fähigkeiten speziell für Stile unter FP8-Kompression wieder her.

es.title: DAQ: Cuantificación consciente del delta para la compresión de pesos de LLM post-entrenamiento
es.summary: El marco de cuantificación post-entrenamiento DAQ conserva el conocimiento post-entrenamiento optimizando la fidelidad direccional del delta de peso en lugar del error de reconstrucción, recuperando capacidades LLM específicas de estilo bajo compresión FP8.

title: Hybrid Associative Memories
summary: HAM layer combines RNNs and self-attention by using attention selectively for information RNNs cannot predict, enabling data-dependent KV cache growth with user-controlled trade-offs between memory cost and performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.22325v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recurrent neural networks (RNNs) and self-attention are both widely used sequence-mixing layers that maintain an internal memory. However, this memory is constructed using two orthogonal mechanisms: RNNs compress the entire past into a fixed-size state, whereas self-attention's state stores every past time step growing its state (the KV cache) linearly with the sequence length. This results in orthogonal strengths and weaknesses. Self-attention layers excel at retrieving information in the context but have large memory and computational costs, while RNNs are more efficient but degrade over longer contexts and underperform for precise recall tasks. Prior work combining these mechanisms has focused primarily on naively interleaving them to reduce computational cost without regard to their complementary mechanisms. We propose the Hybrid Associative Memory (HAM) layer, which combines self-attention and RNNs while leveraging their individual strengths: the RNN compresses the entire sequence, while attention supplements it *only* with information that is difficult for the RNN to predict, which is hence the most valuable information to explicitly store. HAM layers enable data-dependent growth of the KV cache, which can be precisely controlled by the user with a single, continuous threshold. We find that this fine-grained control of the KV cache growth rate has a smooth trade-off with loss and performance. Empirically, we show that our hybrid architecture offers strong, competitive performance relative to RNNs and Transformers even at substantially lower KV-cache usage.

zh.title: 混合联想记忆
zh.summary: HAM层通过选择性地对循环神经网络无法预测的信息使用注意机制，将递归神经网络和自注意力相结合，实现了数据相关的键值缓存增长，并在内存成本和性能之间实现了用户可控的权衡。

fr.title: Mémoires associatives hybrides
fr.summary: La couche HAM combine les RNN et l'auto-attention en utilisant l'attention sélectivement pour les informations que les RNN ne peuvent pas prédire, permettant une croissance de cache KV dépendante des données avec des compromis contrôlés par l'utilisateur entre le coût mémoire et les performances.

de.title: Hybrid assoziative Speicher
de.summary: Die HAM-Schicht kombiniert RNNs und Self-Attention, indem sie Attention selektiv für Informationen verwendet, die RNNs nicht vorhersagen können, und ermöglicht datenenabhängiges KV-Cache-Wachstum mit benutzergesteuerten Kompromissen zwischen Speicherkosten und Leistung.

es.title: Memorias asociativas híbridas
es.summary: La capa HAM combina RNN y auto-atención usando atención selectivamente para información que los RNN no pueden predecir, permitiendo el crecimiento de caché KV dependiente de datos con compensaciones controladas por el usuario entre el costo de memoria y el rendimiento.

title: A Direct Classification Approach for Reliable Wind Ramp Event Forecasting under Severe Class Imbalance
summary: Novel multivariate time-series classification approach for wind power ramp prediction combines majority-class undersampling and ensemble learning to handle severe class imbalance, achieving 85% accuracy on real-world power grid datasets.
content:
arXiv:2603.22326v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Decision support systems are essential for maintaining grid stability in low-carbon power systems, such as wind power plants, by providing real-time alerts to control room operators regarding potential events, including Wind Power Ramp Events (WPREs). These early warnings enable the timely initiation of more detailed system stability assessments and preventive actions. However, forecasting these events is challenging due to the inherent class imbalance in WPRE datasets, where ramp events are less frequent (typically less than 15\% of observed events) compared to normal conditions. Ignoring this characteristic undermines the performance of conventional machine learning models, which often favor the majority class. This paper introduces a novel methodology for WPRE forecasting as a multivariate time series classification task and proposes a data preprocessing strategy that extracts features from recent power observations and masks unavailable ramp information, making it integrable with traditional real-time ramp identification tools. Particularly, the proposed methodology combines majority-class undersampling and ensemble learning to enhance wind ramp event forecasting under class imbalance. Numerical simulations conducted on a real-world dataset demonstrate the superiority of our approach, achieving over 85% accuracy and 88% weighted F1 score, outperforming benchmark classifiers.

zh.title: 严重类不平衡条件下可靠风速升高事件预测的直接分类方法
zh.summary: 新型多变量时间序列分类方法用于风力发电斜升预测，采用多数类欠采样和集成学习相结合的方式处理严重的类不平衡问题，在真实电网数据集上达到85%的准确率。

fr.title: Une approche de classification directe pour une prévision fiable des rampes de vent sous un déséquilibre de classe grave
fr.summary: Une nouvelle approche de classification de séries chronologiques multivariées pour la prédiction des rampes de puissance éolienne combine le sous-échantillonnage de la classe majoritaire et l'apprentissage d'ensemble pour gérer le déséquilibre de classe grave, atteignant une précision de 85% sur des ensembles de données réels du réseau électrique.

de.title: Ein direkter Klassifizierungsansatz für zuverlässige Windrampen-Event-Vorhersage bei schwerem Klassenungleichgewicht
de.summary: Ein neuartiger multivariater Zeitreihen-Klassifizierungsansatz zur Windleistungs-Rampen-Vorhersage kombiniert Unterabtastung der Mehrheitsklasse und Ensemble-Learning, um schweres Klassenungleichgewicht zu handhaben und erreicht 85% Genauigkeit auf echten Stromnetz-Datensätzen.

es.title: Un enfoque de clasificación directa para una predicción confiable de eventos de rampa eólica bajo desequilibrio de clase grave
es.summary: Un nuevo enfoque de clasificación de series de tiempo multivariadas para la predicción de rampas de potencia eólica combina submuestreo de la clase mayoritaria y aprendizaje conjunto para manejar el desequilibrio de clase grave, logrando una precisión del 85% en conjuntos de datos reales de la red eléctrica.

title: AgentSLR: Automating Systematic Literature Reviews in Epidemiology with Agentic AI
summary: AgentSLR agentic pipeline automates end-to-end systematic literature review workflows for epidemiology, achieving human-level performance while reducing review time from 7 weeks to 20 hours (58x speedup) on WHO-designated pathogens.
content:
arXiv:2603.22327v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Systematic literature reviews are essential for synthesizing scientific evidence but are costly, difficult to scale and time-intensive, creating bottlenecks for evidence-based policy. We study whether large language models can automate the complete systematic review workflow, from article retrieval, article screening, data extraction to report synthesis. Applied to epidemiological reviews of nine WHO-designated priority pathogens and validated against expert-curated ground truth, our open-source agentic pipeline (AgentSLR) achieves performance comparable to human researchers while reducing review time from approximately 7 weeks to 20 hours (a 58x speed-up). Our comparison of five frontier models reveals that performance on SLR is driven less by model size or inference cost than by each model's distinctive capabilities. Through human-in-the-loop validation, we identify key failure modes. Our results demonstrate that agentic AI can substantially accelerate scientific evidence synthesis in specialised domains.

zh.title: AgentSLR: 使用智能体AI自动化流行病学系统文献综述
zh.summary: AgentSLR智能体流程自动化流行病学端到端系统文献综述工作流，在WHO指定的病原体上实现与人类相同的性能，同时将综述时间从7周缩减至20小时（加速58倍）。

fr.title: AgentSLR : Automatisation des revues systématiques de la littérature en épidémiologie avec l'IA agentique
fr.summary: Le pipeline agentique AgentSLR automatise les flux de travail des revues systématiques de la littérature de bout en bout en épidémiologie, atteignant des performances au niveau humain tout en réduisant le temps d'examen de 7 semaines à 20 heures (accélération 58x) sur les agents pathogènes désignés par l'OMS.

de.title: AgentSLR: Automatisierung systematischer Literaturübersichten in der Epidemiologie mit agentischer KI
de.summary: Die AgentSLR-Agentur-Pipeline automatisiert End-to-End-Arbeitsabläufe für systematische Literaturübersichten in der Epidemiologie und erreicht menschliches Leistungsniveau, während die Überprüfungszeit von 7 Wochen auf 20 Stunden (58-fache Beschleunigung) bei von der WHO bezeichneten Krankheitserregern reduziert wird.

es.title: AgentSLR: Automatización de revisiones sistemáticas de la literatura en epidemiología con IA agentiva
es.summary: La tubería agentiva AgentSLR automatiza flujos de trabajo de revisión sistemática de literatura de extremo a extremo en epidemiología, logrando un rendimiento a nivel humano mientras reduce el tiempo de revisión de 7 semanas a 20 horas (aceleración de 58x) en patógenos designados por la OMS.

title: Beyond the Mean: Distribution-Aware Loss Functions for Bimodal Regression
summary: Distribution-aware loss functions using Wasserstein and Cramér distances recover bimodal distributions in regression without mixture model instability, reducing uncertainty estimation error by 45% for trustworthy AI applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.22328v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite the strong predictive performance achieved by machine learning models across many application domains, assessing their trustworthiness through reliable estimates of predictive confidence remains a critical challenge. This issue arises in scenarios where the likelihood of error inferred from learned representations follows a bimodal distribution, resulting from the coexistence of confident and ambiguous predictions. Standard regression approaches often struggle to adequately express this predictive uncertainty, as they implicitly assume unimodal Gaussian noise, leading to mean-collapse behavior in such settings. Although Mixture Density Networks (MDNs) can represent different distributions, they suffer from severe optimization instability. We propose a family of distribution-aware loss functions integrating normalized RMSE with Wasserstein and Cram\'er distances. When applied to standard deep regression models, our approach recovers bimodal distributions without the volatility of mixture models. Validated across four experimental stages, our results show that the proposed Wasserstein loss establishes a new Pareto efficiency frontier: matching the stability of standard regression losses like MSE in unimodal tasks while reducing Jensen-Shannon Divergence by 45% on complex bimodal datasets. Our framework strictly dominates MDNs in both fidelity and robustness, offering a reliable tool for aleatoric uncertainty estimation in trustworthy AI systems.

zh.title: 超越均值: 双峰回归的分布感知损失函数
zh.summary: 使用瓦瑟斯坦距离和克莱默距离的分布感知损失函数在不使用混合模型不稳定性的情况下恢复回归中的双峰分布，在可信赖的AI应用中将不确定性估计误差减少45%。

fr.title: Au-delà de la moyenne : fonctions de perte conscientes de la distribution pour la régression bimodale
fr.summary: Les fonctions de perte conscientes de la distribution utilisant les distances de Wasserstein et de Cramér récupèrent les distributions bimodales dans la régression sans instabilité du modèle mixte, réduisant l'erreur d'estimation de l'incertitude de 45% pour les applications d'IA fiables.

de.title: Jenseits des Mittelwerts: Verteilungsbewusste Verlustfunktionen für bimodale Regression
de.summary: Verteilungsbewusste Verlustfunktionen unter Verwendung von Wasserstein- und Cramér-Abständen stellen bimodale Verteilungen in der Regression ohne Mischmodell-Instabilität wieder her und reduzieren den Unsicherheitsschätzungsfehler um 45% für vertrauenswürdige KI-Anwendungen.

es.title: Más allá de la media: funciones de pérdida conscientes de la distribución para regresión bimodal
es.summary: Las funciones de pérdida conscientes de la distribución que utilizan distancias de Wasserstein y Cramér recuperan distribuciones bimodales en regresión sin inestabilidad del modelo de mezcla, reduciendo el error de estimación de incertidumbre en un 45% para aplicaciones de IA confiables.

title: Trained Persistent Memory for Frozen Decoder-Only LLMs
summary: Researchers investigate whether trained memory adapters can provide persistent latent-space memory to frozen decoder-only language models. Testing six methods on a frozen GPT-2 shows that architectures with strong priors achieve better memory retention, with performance convergence at higher capacity.
content:
arXiv:2603.22329v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Decoder-only language models are stateless: hidden representations are discarded after every forward pass and nothing persists across sessions. Jeong (2026a) showed that trained memory adapters give a frozen encoder-decoder backbone persistent latent-space memory, building on the lateral-memory framework of Jeong (2026b,c). Here we ask whether the same principle transfers to the decoder-only setting, where no cross-attention pathway exists and memory must enter through self-attention alone. We adapt six methods -- prefix, parallel cross-attention, KV extension, Hebbian memory, context-gated branch, and slot-based sparse write -- to a frozen GPT-2, training only a small adapter $\theta_{mem}$. The write rule is shared; only the read injection changes from decoder cross-attention to self-attention KV prefix or parallel branch. On LoCoMo we find a striking inductive-bias dichotomy: at $1\times$ capacity, three methods with strong architectural priors -- cross-attention (M.2), Hebbian (M.4), and slot write (M.6) -- achieve retained-memory scores of $7-18\%$ and knowledge gains $\Delta K$ of $7-10$, while the other three fail ($< 0.4\%$). At $10\times$ capacity all six converge, showing the gap is architectural, not fundamental. Together with the encoder-decoder results of Jeong (2026a) and the brain-inspired modules of Jeong (2026b,c), these findings establish persistent latent-space memory as a general paradigm spanning major transformer families.

zh.title: 冻结仅解码器语言模型的训练持久内存
zh.summary: 研究人员调查训练的内存适配器是否可以为冻结的仅解码器语言模型提供持久的潜在空间内存。在冻结的GPT-2上测试六种方法表明，具有强先验的架构实现更好的内存保留，性能在更高容量下收敛。

fr.title: Mémoire persistante entraînée pour les LLM décodeur uniquement figés
fr.summary: Les chercheurs enquêtent si les adaptateurs de mémoire entraînés peuvent fournir une mémoire persistante de l'espace latent aux modèles de langage décodeur uniquement figés. Les tests de six méthodes sur un GPT-2 gelé montrent que les architectures avec de forts a priori obtiennent une meilleure rétention de mémoire, avec convergence de performance à capacité plus élevée.

de.title: Trainierte persistente Speicherung für gefrorene Decoder-Only LLMs
de.summary: Forscher untersuchen, ob trainierte Speicheradapter persistenten latenten Speicher für gefrorene Decoder-Only-Sprachmodelle bereitstellen können. Tests von sechs Methoden auf einem gefrorenen GPT-2 zeigen, dass Architekturen mit starken Priors bessere Speicherretention erreichen, mit Leistungskonvergenz bei höherer Kapazität.

es.title: Memoria persistente entrenada para LLM de solo decodificador congelados
es.summary: Los investigadores investigan si los adaptadores de memoria entrenados pueden proporcionar memoria persistente del espacio latente a los modelos de lenguaje de solo decodificador congelados. Las pruebas de seis métodos en un GPT-2 congelado muestran que las arquitecturas con fuertes priors logran una mejor retención de memoria, con convergencia de rendimiento a mayor capacidad.

title: Conformal Risk Control for Safety-Critical Wildfire Evacuation Mapping: A Comparative Study of Tabular, Spatial, and Graph-Based Models
summary: Researchers apply conformal risk control to wildfire prediction models to provide formal safety guarantees on false negative rates. Testing across three model families reveals that model architecture determines evacuation efficiency while conformal risk control ensures safety.
content:
arXiv:2603.22331v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Every wildfire prediction model deployed today shares a dangerous property: none of these methods provides formal guarantees on how much fire spread is missed. Despite extensive work on wildfire spread prediction using deep learning, no prior study has applied distribution-free safety guarantees to this domain, leaving evacuation planners reliant on probability thresholds with no formal assurance. We address this gap by presenting, to our knowledge, the first application of conformal risk control (CRC) to wildfire spread prediction, providing finite-sample guarantees on false negative rate (FNR <= 0.05). We expose a stark failure: across three model families of increasing complexity (tabular: LightGBM, AUROC 0.854; convolutional: Tiny U-Net, AUROC 0.969; and graph-based: Hybrid ResGNN-UNet, AUROC 0.964), standard thresholds capture only 7-72% of true fire spread. CRC eliminates this failure uniformly. Our central finding is that model architecture determines evacuation efficiency, while CRC determines safety: both spatial models with CRC achieve approximately 95% fire coverage while flagging only approximately 15% of total pixels, making them 4.2x more efficient than LightGBM, while the graph model's additional complexity over a simple U-Net yields no meaningful efficiency gain. We propose a shift-aware three-way CRC framework that assigns SAFE/MONITOR/EVACUATE zones for operational triage, and characterize a fundamental limitation of prevalence-weighted bounds under extreme class imbalance (approximately 5% fire prevalence). All models, calibration code, and evaluation pipelines are released for reproducibility.

zh.title: 保形风险控制在安全关键型野火撤离地图的应用：表格型、空间型和图基型模型的比较研究
zh.summary: 研究人员将保形风险控制应用于野火预测模型，以提供关于假阴性率的正式安全保证。对三个模型族的测试表明，模型架构决定了撤离效率，而保形风险控制确保了安全性。

fr.title: Contrôle des Risques Conformes pour la Cartographie Sécuritaire de l'Évacuation des Feux de Forêt : Étude Comparative des Modèles Tabulaires, Spatiaux et Basés sur des Graphes
fr.summary: Les chercheurs appliquent le contrôle des risques conformes aux modèles de prédiction des feux de forêt pour fournir des garanties de sécurité formelles sur les taux de faux négatifs. Les tests sur trois familles de modèles révèlent que l'architecture du modèle détermine l'efficacité de l'évacuation tandis que le contrôle des risques conformes assure la sécurité.

de.title: Konforme Risikokontrolle für Sicherheitskritische Evakuierungskarten bei Waldbränden: Eine Vergleichende Studie von Tabellarischen, Räumlichen und Graphengestützten Modellen
de.summary: Forscher wenden konforme Risikokontrolle auf Waldbrrandvorhersagemodelle an, um formale Sicherheitsgarantien für falsch-negative Raten zu bieten. Tests über drei Modellfamilien zeigen, dass die Modellarchitektur die Evakuierungseffizienz bestimmt, während konforme Risikokontrolle die Sicherheit gewährleistet.

es.title: Control Conforme de Riesgos para Mapeo de Evacuación de Incendios Forestales Críticos para la Seguridad: Un Estudio Comparativo de Modelos Tabulares, Espaciales y Basados en Gráficos
es.summary: Los investigadores aplican control conforme de riesgos a modelos de predicción de incendios forestales para proporcionar garantías de seguridad formales en tasas de falsos negativos. Las pruebas en tres familias de modelos revelan que la arquitectura del modelo determina la eficiencia de evacuación mientras que el control conforme de riesgos asegura la seguridad.

title: Large Language Models for Missing Data Imputation: Understanding Behavior, Hallucination Effects, and Control Mechanisms
summary: A comprehensive benchmarking study evaluates five LLMs against six traditional imputation methods across 29 datasets under various missing data mechanisms. Advanced LLMs like Gemini 3.0 Flash and Claude 4.5 Sonnet excel on real-world data but underperform traditional methods on synthetic data, suggesting their effectiveness derives from semantic context.
content:
arXiv:2603.22332v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Data imputation is a cornerstone technique for handling missing values in real-world datasets, which are often plagued by missingness. Despite recent progress, prior studies on Large Language Models-based imputation remain limited by scalability challenges, restricted cross-model comparisons, and evaluations conducted on small or domain-specific datasets. Furthermore, heterogeneous experimental protocols and inconsistent treatment of missingness mechanisms (MCAR, MAR, and MNAR) hinder systematic benchmarking across methods. This work investigates the robustness of Large Language Models for missing data imputation in tabular datasets using a zero-shot prompt engineering approach. To this end, we present a comprehensive benchmarking study comparing five widely used LLMs against six state-of-the-art imputation baselines. The experimental design evaluates these methods across 29 datasets (including nine synthetic datasets) under MCAR, MAR, and MNAR mechanisms, with missing rates of up to 20\%. The results demonstrate that leading LLMs, particularly Gemini 3.0 Flash and Claude 4.5 Sonnet, consistently achieve superior performance on real-world open-source datasets compared to traditional methods. However, this advantage appears to be closely tied to the models' prior exposure to domain-specific patterns learned during pre-training on internet-scale corpora. In contrast, on synthetic datasets, traditional methods such as MICE outperform LLMs, suggesting that LLM effectiveness is driven by semantic context rather than purely statistical reconstruction. Furthermore, we identify a clear trade-off: while LLMs excel in imputation quality, they incur significantly higher computational time and monetary costs. Overall, this study provides a large-scale comparative analysis, positioning LLMs as promising semantics-driven imputers for complex tabular data.

zh.title: 大型语言模型在缺失数据插补中的应用：理解行为、幻觉效应和控制机制
zh.summary: 一项全面的基准测试研究评估了五个大型语言模型与六种传统插补方法在29个数据集上的性能，这些数据集采用各种缺失数据机制。先进的大型语言模型如Gemini 3.0 Flash和Claude 4.5 Sonnet在真实数据上表现出色，但在合成数据上的表现不如传统方法，这表明它们的有效性源于语义背景。

fr.title: Modèles de Langage de Grande Taille pour l'Imputation de Données Manquantes : Comprendre le Comportement, les Effets d'Hallucination et les Mécanismes de Contrôle
fr.summary: Une étude comparative complète évalue cinq modèles de langage de grande taille par rapport à six méthodes d'imputation traditionnelles sur 29 ensembles de données sous diverses mécanismes de données manquantes. Les modèles de langage avancés comme Gemini 3.0 Flash et Claude 4.5 Sonnet excellent sur les données du monde réel mais sous-performent par rapport aux méthodes traditionnelles sur les données synthétiques, ce qui suggère que leur efficacité provient du contexte sémantique.

de.title: Große Sprachmodelle zur Imputation Fehlender Daten: Verständnis von Verhalten, Halluzinations-Effekten und Kontrollmechanismen
de.summary: Eine umfassende Benchmark-Studie evaluiert fünf große Sprachmodelle gegen sechs traditionelle Imputationsmethoden über 29 Datensätze unter verschiedenen Mechanismen fehlender Daten. Fortgeschrittene große Sprachmodelle wie Gemini 3.0 Flash und Claude 4.5 Sonnet brillieren bei realen Daten, aber unterperformen traditionelle Methoden bei synthetischen Daten, was darauf hindeutet, dass ihre Wirksamkeit vom semantischen Kontext herrührt.

es.title: Modelos de Lenguaje Grande para Imputación de Datos Faltantes: Entendimiento del Comportamiento, Efectos de Alucinación y Mecanismos de Control
es.summary: Un estudio integral de evaluación comparativa evalúa cinco modelos de lenguaje grande contra seis métodos de imputación tradicionales en 29 conjuntos de datos bajo varios mecanismos de datos faltantes. Los modelos de lenguaje avanzados como Gemini 3.0 Flash y Claude 4.5 Sonnet sobresalen en datos del mundo real pero tienen un desempeño inferior a los métodos tradicionales en datos sintéticos, sugiriendo que su efectividad proviene del contexto semántico.

title: Graph Signal Processing Meets Mamba2: Adaptive Filter Bank via Delta Modulation
summary: Researchers propose HADES, a Graph Signal Processing framework that reinterprets Mamba2 as an adaptive filter bank using hierarchical architecture. The approach achieves comparable performance to Mamba2 while using only 58.9% of the original parameters.
content:
arXiv:2603.22333v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: State-space models (SSMs) offer efficient alternatives to attention with linear-time recurrence. Mamba2, a recent SSM-based language model, uses selective input gating and a multi-head structure, enabling parallel computation and strong benchmark performance. However, its multi-head recurrence operates independently without structured utilization or analysis. In this work, we propose a novel method called Hierarchical ADaptive filter bank for Efficient SSMs (HADES), a Graph Signal Processing (GSP)-inspired framework that reinterprets Mamba2 as an adaptive filter bank on a line graph. Our hierarchical architecture introduces two filter types: shared filters for global low-pass behavior and expert filters for local high-pass behavior, achieved through structured bias on the parameter {\Delta}. HADES achieves comparable performance to baseline models including Mamba2 across various benchmarks in language modeling, commonsense reasoning, and long-context retrieval, while using only 58.9% of the original parameters. In this regard, HADES bridges GSP and neural sequence modeling, enabling efficient, hierarchical, and interpretable filtering within state-space models.

zh.title: 图信号处理遇见Mamba2：通过增量调制的自适应滤波器组
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了HADES，一个图信号处理框架，通过分层架构将Mamba2重新解释为自适应滤波器组。该方法实现了与Mamba2相当的性能，同时仅使用了原始参数的58.9%。

fr.title: Traitement des Signaux Graphiques Rencontre Mamba2 : Banc de Filtres Adaptatif par Modulation Delta
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent HADES, un framework de Traitement des Signaux Graphiques qui réinterprète Mamba2 comme un banc de filtres adaptatif utilisant une architecture hiérarchique. L'approche atteint des performances comparables à Mamba2 en utilisant seulement 58,9% des paramètres originaux.

de.title: Graphensignalverarbeitung Trifft Mamba2: Adaptives Filterbank-Design durch Delta-Modulation
de.summary: Forscher schlagen HADES vor, ein Graphensignalverarbeitungs-Framework, das Mamba2 mithilfe einer hierarchischen Architektur als adaptives Filterbank neu interpretiert. Der Ansatz erreicht eine vergleichbare Leistung wie Mamba2, während nur 58,9% der ursprünglichen Parameter verwendet werden.

es.title: Procesamiento de Señales de Gráficos Conoce a Mamba2: Banco de Filtros Adaptativo por Modulación Delta
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen HADES, un marco de Procesamiento de Señales de Gráficos que reinterpreta Mamba2 como un banco de filtros adaptativo utilizando arquitectura jerárquica. El enfoque logra un desempeño comparable a Mamba2 mientras utiliza solo el 58,9% de los parámetros originales.

title: Causal Direct Preference Optimization for Distributionally Robust Generative Recommendation
summary: Researchers identify that Direct Preference Optimization tends to amplify spurious correlations from environmental confounders, reducing generalization in out-of-distribution scenarios. They propose CausalDPO, which incorporates causal invariance learning to improve robust recommendation generation across diverse environments.
content:
arXiv:2603.22335v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) guides large language models (LLMs) to generate recommendations aligned with user historical behavior distributions by minimizing preference alignment loss. However, our systematic empirical research and theoretical analysis reveal that DPO tends to amplify spurious correlations caused by environmental confounders during the alignment process, significantly undermining the generalization capability of LLM-based generative recommendation methods in out of distribution (OOD) scenarios. To mitigate this issue, we propose CausalDPO, an extension of DPO that incorporates a causal invariance learning mechanism. This method introduces a backdoor adjustment strategy during the preference alignment phase to eliminate interference from environmental confounders, explicitly models the latent environmental distribution using a soft clustering approach, and enhances robust consistency across diverse environments through invariance constraints. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that CausalDPO can effectively capture users stable preference structures across multiple environments, thereby improving the OOD generalization performance of LLM-based recommendation models. We conduct extensive experiments under four representative distribution shift settings to validate the effectiveness of CausalDPO, achieving an average performance improvement of 17.17% across four evaluation metrics.

zh.title: 用于分布鲁棒性生成推荐的因果直接偏好优化
zh.summary: 研究人员发现直接偏好优化倾向于放大环境混杂因素的虚假相关性，从而降低分布外场景中的泛化能力。他们提出CausalDPO，该方法结合因果不变性学习，以改进不同环境中稳健推荐生成。

fr.title: Optimisation des Préférences Directes Causales pour la Recommandation Générative Robuste en Distribution
fr.summary: Les chercheurs identifient que l'Optimisation des Préférences Directes tend à amplifier les corrélations spurieuses provenant des facteurs de confusion environnementaux, réduisant la généralisation dans les scénarios hors distribution. Ils proposent CausalDPO, qui intègre l'apprentissage d'invariance causale pour améliorer la génération robuste de recommandations dans des environnements divers.

de.title: Kausale Direkte Präferenzoptimierung für distributional robuste generative Empfehlungen
de.summary: Forscher stellen fest, dass Direct Preference Optimization dazu neigt, spurious Korrelationen von Umweltverwirrfaktoren zu verstärken und die Verallgemeinerung in Out-of-Distribution-Szenarien zu verringern. Sie schlagen CausalDPO vor, das kausales Invarianzlernen integriert, um eine robuste Empfehlungsgenerierung in verschiedenen Umgebungen zu verbessern.

es.title: Optimización Causal de Preferencias Directas para Recomendación Generativa Robusta en Distribución
es.summary: Los investigadores identifican que la Optimización de Preferencias Directas tiende a amplificar correlaciones espurias de confusores ambientales, reduciendo la generalización en escenarios fuera de distribución. Proponen CausalDPO, que incorpora aprendizaje de invariancia causal para mejorar la generación robusta de recomendaciones en diversos entornos.

title: Graphs RAG at Scale: Beyond Retrieval-Augmented Generation With Labeled Property Graphs and Resource Description Framework for Complex and Unknown Search Spaces
summary: A new Graph RAG framework leverages Labeled Property Graphs and Resource Description Framework to overcome limitations of traditional RAG with unknown or semi-structured search spaces. The approach includes a text-to-Cypher framework achieving over 90% accuracy and demonstrates superior performance in complex reasoning tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.22340v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recent advances in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) have revolutionized knowledge-intensive tasks, yet traditional RAG methods struggle when the search space is unknown or when documents are semi-structured or structured. We introduce a novel end-to-end Graph RAG framework that leverages both Labeled Property Graph (LPG) and Resource Description Framework (RDF) architectures to overcome these limitations. Our approach enables dynamic document retrieval without the need to pre-specify the number of documents and eliminates inefficient reranking. We propose an innovative method for converting documents into RDF triplets using JSON key-value pairs, facilitating seamless integration of semi-structured data. Additionally, we present a text to Cypher framework for LPG, achieving over 90% accuracy in real-time translation of text queries to Cypher, enabling fast and reliable query generation suitable for online applications. Our empirical evaluation demonstrates that Graph RAG significantly outperforms traditional embedding-based RAG in accuracy, response quality, and reasoning, especially for complex, semi-structured tasks. These findings establish Graph RAG as a transformative solution for next-generation retrieval-augmented systems.

zh.title: 大规模图RAG：超越检索增强生成，采用标记属性图和资源描述框架处理复杂和未知搜索空间
zh.summary: 一个新的图RAG框架利用标记属性图和资源描述框架来克服传统RAG在未知或半结构化搜索空间中的限制。该方法包括一个文本到Cypher框架，准确率超过90%，在复杂推理任务中展示了优异的性能。

fr.title: Graphs RAG à l'Échelle : Au-delà de la Génération Augmentée par Récupération avec Graphes de Propriétés Étiquetés et Cadre de Description des Ressources pour des Espaces de Recherche Complexes et Inconnus
fr.summary: Un nouveau cadre Graph RAG exploite les Graphes de Propriétés Étiquetés et le Cadre de Description des Ressources pour surmonter les limitations du RAG traditionnel avec des espaces de recherche inconnus ou semi-structurés. L'approche inclut un cadre texte-vers-Cypher atteignant une précision supérieure à 90% et démontre des performances supérieures dans les tâches de raisonnement complexe.

de.title: Graphs RAG im Maßstab: Über Retrieval-Augmented Generation hinaus mit Labeled Property Graphs und Resource Description Framework für komplexe und unbekannte Suchräume
de.summary: Ein neues Graph-RAG-Framework nutzt Labeled Property Graphs und Resource Description Framework, um Einschränkungen des traditionellen RAG bei unbekannten oder halbstrukturierten Suchräumen zu überwinden. Der Ansatz umfasst ein Text-zu-Cypher-Framework mit über 90% Genauigkeit und demonstriert überlegene Leistung bei komplexen Reasoning-Aufgaben.

es.title: Graphs RAG a Escala: Más Allá de la Generación Aumentada por Recuperación con Grafos de Propiedades Etiquetados y Marco de Descripción de Recursos para Espacios de Búsqueda Complejos e Desconocidos
es.summary: Un nuevo marco Graph RAG aprovecha los Grafos de Propiedades Etiquetados y el Marco de Descripción de Recursos para superar las limitaciones del RAG tradicional con espacios de búsqueda desconocidos o semiestructurados. El enfoque incluye un marco de texto a Cypher que logra una precisión superior al 90% y demuestra un rendimiento superior en tareas de razonamiento complejo.

title: T-MAP: Red-Teaming LLM Agents with Trajectory-aware Evolutionary Search
summary: Researchers introduce T-MAP, a trajectory-aware evolutionary search method designed to discover adversarial prompts that exploit vulnerabilities in LLM agents. Testing against frontier models shows T-MAP substantially outperforms baselines in generating attacks that bypass safety guardrails.
content:
arXiv:2603.22341v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While prior red-teaming efforts have focused on eliciting harmful text outputs from large language models (LLMs), such approaches fail to capture agent-specific vulnerabilities that emerge through multi-step tool execution, particularly in rapidly growing ecosystems such as the Model Context Protocol (MCP). To address this gap, we propose a trajectory-aware evolutionary search method, T-MAP, which leverages execution trajectories to guide the discovery of adversarial prompts. Our approach enables the automatic generation of attacks that not only bypass safety guardrails but also reliably realize harmful objectives through actual tool interactions. Empirical evaluations across diverse MCP environments demonstrate that T-MAP substantially outperforms baselines in attack realization rate (ARR) and remains effective against frontier models, including GPT-5.2, Gemini-3-Pro, Qwen3.5, and GLM-5, thereby revealing previously underexplored vulnerabilities in autonomous LLM agents.

zh.title: T-MAP：通过轨迹感知进化搜索进行LLM代理的红队测试
zh.summary: 研究人员引入T-MAP，一种轨迹感知的进化搜索方法，旨在发现利用LLM代理中漏洞的对抗性提示。对前沿模型的测试表明，T-MAP在生成绕过安全防护栏的攻击方面明显优于基线方法。

fr.title: T-MAP : Red-Teaming des Agents LLM avec Recherche Évolutionnaire Consciente de la Trajectoire
fr.summary: Les chercheurs introduisent T-MAP, une méthode de recherche évolutionnaire consciente de la trajectoire conçue pour découvrir les invites adversariales qui exploitent les vulnérabilités des agents LLM. Les tests contre les modèles frontier montrent que T-MAP surpasse considérablement les approches de base dans la génération d'attaques qui contournent les garde-fous de sécurité.

de.title: T-MAP: Red-Teaming von LLM-Agenten mit Trajektorienbewusstem Evolutionärem Suche
de.summary: Forscher führen T-MAP ein, eine trajektorienbewusste Evolutionärsuchenmethode, die entwickelt wurde, um gegnerische Prompts zu entdecken, die Anfälligkeiten in LLM-Agenten ausnutzen. Tests gegen Frontier-Modelle zeigen, dass T-MAP Baselines bei der Generierung von Angriffen, die Sicherheitsvorkehrungen umgehen, erheblich übertrifft.

es.title: T-MAP: Pruebas de Seguridad de Agentes LLM con Búsqueda Evolutiva Consciente de Trayectorias
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan T-MAP, un método de búsqueda evolutiva consciente de trayectorias diseñado para descubrir indicaciones adversarias que explotan vulnerabilidades en agentes LLM. Las pruebas contra modelos fronterizos muestran que T-MAP supera sustancialmente los enfoques basales en la generación de ataques que eludan los salvaguardias de seguridad.

title: First-Mover Bias in Gradient Boosting Explanations: Mechanism, Detection, and Resolution
summary: Researchers isolate first-mover bias in gradient boosting, where sequential residual fitting creates path-dependent feature importance concentration that causes SHAP-based feature ranking instability. They propose DASH and other methods that restore explanation stability by breaking the sequential dependency chain.
content:
arXiv:2603.22346v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We isolate and empirically characterize first-mover bias -- a path-dependent concentration of feature importance caused by sequential residual fitting in gradient boosting -- as a specific mechanistic cause of the well-known instability of SHAP-based feature rankings under multicollinearity. When correlated features compete for early splits, gradient boosting creates a self-reinforcing advantage for whichever feature is selected first: subsequent trees inherit modified residuals that favor the incumbent, concentrating SHAP importance on an arbitrary feature rather than distributing it across the correlated group. Scaling up a single model amplifies this effect -- a Large Single Model with the same total tree count as our method produces the worst explanations of any approach tested. We demonstrate that model independence is sufficient to resolve first-mover bias in the linear regime, and remains the most effective mitigation under nonlinear data-generating processes. Both our proposed method, DASH (Diversified Aggregation of SHAP), and simple seed-averaging (Stochastic Retrain) restore stability by breaking the sequential dependency chain, confirming that the operative mechanism is independence between explained models. At rho=0.9, both achieve stability=0.977, while the single-best workflow degrades to 0.958 and the Large Single Model to 0.938. On the Breast Cancer dataset, DASH improves stability from 0.32 to 0.93 (+0.61) against a tree-count-matched baseline. DASH additionally provides two diagnostic tools -- the Feature Stability Index (FSI) and Importance-Stability (IS) Plot -- that detect first-mover bias without ground truth, enabling practitioners to audit explanation reliability before acting on feature rankings. Software and reproducible benchmarks are available at https://github.com/DrakeCaraker/dash-shap.

zh.title: 梯度提升中的首位偏差：机制、检测和解决方案
zh.summary: 研究人员在梯度提升中识别了首位偏差，其中顺序残差拟合会创建路径相关的特征重要性集中，导致基于SHAP的特征排名不稳定。他们提出DASH和其他方法通过打破顺序依赖链来恢复解释稳定性。

fr.title: Biais du premier arrivant dans les explications du gradient boosting : mécanisme, détection et résolution
fr.summary: Les chercheurs isolent le biais du premier arrivant dans le gradient boosting, où l'ajustement séquentiel des résidus crée une concentration de l'importance des caractéristiques dépendante du chemin qui provoque une instabilité du classement des caractéristiques basée sur SHAP. Ils proposent DASH et d'autres méthodes qui restaurent la stabilité des explications en cassant la chaîne de dépendance séquentielle.

de.title: First-Mover Bias in Gradient Boosting Erklärungen: Mechanismus, Erkennung und Lösung
de.summary: Forscher isolieren First-Mover Bias in Gradient Boosting, wo sequenzielles Residuen-Fitting eine pfadabhängige Konzentration der Merkmalsbedeutung schafft, die zu Instabilität der SHAP-basierten Merkmalseinstufung führt. Sie schlagen DASH und andere Methoden vor, die die Stabilität der Erklärungen durch Aufbrechen der sequenziellen Abhängigkeitskette wiederherstellen.

es.title: Sesgo del primer movimiento en explicaciones de aumento de gradiente: mecanismo, detección y resolución
es.summary: Los investigadores aíslan el sesgo del primer movimiento en el aumento de gradiente, donde el ajuste secuencial de residuos crea una concentración de importancia de características dependiente de la ruta que causa inestabilidad en la clasificación de características basada en SHAP. Proponen DASH y otros métodos que restauran la estabilidad de las explicaciones rompiendo la cadena de dependencia secuencial.

title: WIST: Web-Grounded Iterative Self-Play Tree for Domain-Targeted Reasoning Improvement
summary: WIST is a framework for domain-targeted reasoning improvement that learns from the open web without requiring curated domain corpora. Testing across multiple model sizes shows consistent improvements, with gains reaching +9.8 points on some benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.22352v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recent progress in reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) offers a practical path to self-improvement of language models, but existing methods face a key trade-off: endogenous self-play can drift over iterations, while corpus-grounded approaches rely on curated data environments. We present \textbf{WIST}, a \textbf{W}eb-grounded \textbf{I}terative \textbf{S}elf-play \textbf{T}ree framework for domain-targeted reasoning improvement that learns directly from the open web without requiring any pre-arranged domain corpus. WIST incrementally expands a domain tree for exploration, and retrieves and cleans path-consistent web corpus to construct a controllable training environment. It then performs Challenger--Solver self-play with verifiable rewards, and feeds learnability signals back to update node posteriors and guide subsequent exploration through an adaptive curriculum. Across four backbones, WIST consistently improves over the base models and typically outperforms both purely endogenous self-evolution and corpus-grounded self-play baselines, with the Overall gains reaching \textbf{+9.8} (\textit{Qwen3-4B-Base}) and \textbf{+9.7} (\textit{OctoThinker-8B}). WIST is also domain-steerable, improving \textit{Qwen3-8B-Base} by \textbf{+14.79} in medicine and \textit{Qwen3-4B-Base} by \textbf{+5.28} on PhyBench. Ablations further confirm the importance of WIST's key components for stable open-web learning. Our Code is available at https://github.com/lfy-123/WIST.

zh.title: WIST：用于领域目标推理改进的网络化迭代自玩树
zh.summary: WIST是一个框架，用于领域目标推理改进，从开放网络学习而无需精心策划的领域语料库。在多个模型规模的测试显示持续改进，某些基准上的收益达到+9.8分。

fr.title: WIST : Arbre d'auto-jeu itératif ancré sur le web pour l'amélioration du raisonnement ciblé au domaine
fr.summary: WIST est un cadre pour l'amélioration du raisonnement ciblé au domaine qui apprend du web ouvert sans nécessiter de corpus de domaine organisés. Les tests sur plusieurs tailles de modèles montrent des améliorations cohérentes, avec des gains atteignant +9,8 points sur certains benchmarks.

de.title: WIST: Web-basierter iterativer selbstspielender Baum zur Verbesserung des domänengerichteten Denkens
de.summary: WIST ist ein Framework zur Verbesserung des domänengerichteten Denkens, das aus dem offenen Web lernt, ohne dass kuratierte Domain-Korpora erforderlich sind. Tests über mehrere Modellgrößen hinweg zeigen konsistente Verbesserungen, mit Gewinnen von bis zu +9,8 Punkten bei einigen Benchmarks.

es.title: WIST: Árbol de autojuego iterativo basado en web para mejora del razonamiento dirigido por dominio
es.summary: WIST es un marco para la mejora del razonamiento dirigido por dominio que aprende de la web abierta sin requerir corpus de dominio curados. Las pruebas en múltiples tamaños de modelo muestran mejoras consistentes, con ganancias que alcanzan +9,8 puntos en algunos puntos de referencia.

title: Bridging neuroscience and AI: adaptive, culturally sensitive technologies transforming aphasia rehabilitation
summary: Researchers examine how AI and neuroscience insights can improve aphasia rehabilitation, addressing barriers like limited therapist availability and lack of culturally relevant tools. The work presents digital therapy prototypes designed for local linguistic diversity and outlines future directions for personalized rehabilitation.
content:
arXiv:2603.22357v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Aphasia, a language impairment primarily resulting from stroke or brain injury, profoundly disrupts communication and everyday functioning. Despite advances in speech therapy, barriers such as limited therapist availability and the scarcity of personalized, culturally relevant tools continue to hinder optimal rehabilitation outcomes. This paper reviews recent developments in neurocognitive research and language technologies that contribute to the diagnosis and therapy of aphasia. Drawing on findings from our ethnographic field study, we introduce two digital therapy prototypes designed to reflect local linguistic diversity and enhance patient engagement. We also show how insights from neuroscience and the local context guided the design of these tools to better meet patient and therapist needs. Our work highlights the potential of adaptive, AI-enhanced assistive technologies to complement conventional therapy and broaden access to therapy. We conclude by outlining future research directions for advancing personalized and scalable aphasia rehabilitation.

zh.title: 联合神经科学和人工智能：自适应、文化敏感技术改变失语症康复
zh.summary: 研究人员研究了人工智能和神经科学见解如何改进失语症康复，解决治疗师可用性有限和缺乏文化相关工具等障碍。该工作展示了为本地语言多样性设计的数字疗法原型，并为个性化康复制定了未来方向。

fr.title: Combler les neurosciences et l'IA : des technologies adaptatives et sensibles à la culture transforment la réadaptation de l'aphasie
fr.summary: Les chercheurs examinent comment les perspectives de l'IA et des neurosciences peuvent améliorer la réadaptation de l'aphasie, en abordant les obstacles tels que la disponibilité limitée des thérapeutes et l'absence d'outils culturellement pertinents. Le travail présente des prototypes de thérapie numérique conçus pour la diversité linguistique locale et décrit les orientations futures de la réadaptation personnalisée.

de.title: Neurowissenschaft und KI verbinden: adaptive, kulturell sensible Technologien transformieren die Aphasie-Rehabilitation
de.summary: Forscher untersuchen, wie KI- und Neurowissenschaftserkenntnisse die Aphasie-Rehabilitation verbessern können, und befassen sich mit Hindernissen wie begrenzter Verfügbarkeit von Therapeuten und dem Mangel an kulturell relevanten Instrumenten. Die Arbeit präsentiert digitale Therapieprototypen, die für lokale sprachliche Vielfalt konzipiert sind, und beschreibt zukünftige Richtungen für personalisierte Rehabilitation.

es.title: Cerrando la brecha entre neurociencia e IA: tecnologías adaptativas y culturalmente sensibles transforman la rehabilitación de la afasia
es.summary: Los investigadores examinan cómo las perspectivas de la IA y la neurociencia pueden mejorar la rehabilitación de la afasia, abordando barreras como la disponibilidad limitada de terapeutas y la falta de herramientas culturalmente relevantes. El trabajo presenta prototipos de terapia digital diseñados para la diversidad lingüística local y describe direcciones futuras para la rehabilitación personalizada.

title: Unveiling the Mechanism of Continuous Representation Full-Waveform Inversion: A Wave Based Neural Tangent Kernel Framework
summary: Researchers develop a theoretical framework using neural tangent kernels to understand why continuous representation full-waveform inversion using neural networks alleviates dependence on initial model accuracy. The analysis reveals non-constant eigenvalue decay in wave-based NTK, explaining both the method's robustness advantages and slower high-frequency convergence. They propose improved variants including a hybrid approach combining neural representations with multi-resolution grids, demonstrating superior performance on geophysical exploration benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.22362v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Full-waveform inversion (FWI) estimates physical parameters in the wave equation from limited measurements and has been widely applied in geophysical exploration, medical imaging, and non-destructive testing. Conventional FWI methods are limited by their notorious sensitivity to the accuracy of the initial models. Recent progress in continuous representation FWI (CR-FWI) demonstrates that representing parameter models with a coordinate-based neural network, such as implicit neural representation (INR), can mitigate the dependence on initial models. However, its underlying mechanism remains unclear, and INR-based FWI shows slower high-frequency convergence. In this work, we investigate the general CR-FWI framework and develop a unified theoretical understanding by extending the neural tangent kernel (NTK) for FWI to establish a wave-based NTK framework. Unlike standard NTK, our analysis reveals that wave-based NTK is not constant, both at initialization and during training, due to the inherent nonlinearity of FWI. We further show that the eigenvalue decay behavior of the wave-based NTK can explain why CR-FWI alleviates the dependency on initial models and shows slower high-frequency convergence. Building on these insights, we propose several CR-FWI methods with tailored eigenvalue decay properties for FWI, including a novel hybrid representation combining INR and multi-resolution grid (termed IG-FWI) that achieves a more balanced trade-off between robustness and high-frequency convergence rate. Applications in geophysical exploration on Marmousi, 2D SEG/EAGE Salt and Overthrust, 2004 BP model, and the more realistic 2014 Chevron models show the superior performance of our proposed methods compared to conventional FWI and existing INR-based FWI methods.

zh.title: 连续表示全波形反演机制揭示：基于波的神经切核框架
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一个使用神经切核的理论框架，以理解为什么使用神经网络的连续表示全波形反演能减轻对初始模型精度的依赖。分析揭示了基于波的NTK中的非常数特征值衰减，解释了该方法的鲁棒性优势和较慢的高频收敛。他们提出了改进的变体，包括将神经表示与多分辨率网格相结合的混合方法，在地球物理勘探基准测试中表现出更好的性能。

fr.title: Dévoilement du Mécanisme d'Inversion Cinématique de Représentation Continue : Cadre de Noyau Tangent Neuronal Basé sur les Ondes
fr.summary: Les chercheurs développent un cadre théorique utilisant des noyaux tangents neuraux pour comprendre pourquoi l'inversion cinématique à représentation continue utilisant des réseaux de neurones atténue la dépendance à la précision du modèle initial. L'analyse révèle une décroissance des valeurs propres non constante dans le NTK basé sur les ondes, expliquant à la fois les avantages de robustesse de la méthode et la convergence plus lente des hautes fréquences. Ils proposent des variantes améliorées, notamment une approche hybride combinant des représentations neurales avec des grilles multirésolution, démontrant des performances supérieures sur les repères d'exploration géophysique.

de.title: Enthüllung des Mechanismus der kontinuierlichen Darstellung von Vollellenumwandlung: Ein wellenbasiertes neuronales Tangente-Kernel-Framework
de.summary: Forscher entwickeln ein theoretisches Rahmenwerk unter Verwendung von neuronalen Tangenten-Kerneln, um zu verstehen, warum die kontinuierliche Darstellung der Vollellenumwandlung unter Verwendung von neuronalen Netzen die Abhängigkeit von der Genauigkeit des Anfangsmodells verringert. Die Analyse zeigt einen nicht konstanten Eigenwertverlauf im wellengestützten NTK und erklärt sowohl die Robustheitsvorteile der Methode als auch die langsamere Konvergenz hoher Frequenzen. Sie schlagen verbesserte Varianten vor, darunter einen hybriden Ansatz, der neuronale Darstellungen mit Multiresolutionsgittern kombiniert und überlegene Leistung bei geophysikalischen Explorationsbenchmarks demonstriert.

es.title: Revelación del Mecanismo de Inversión de Forma de Onda Completa de Representación Continua: Marco de Núcleo Tangente Neuronal Basado en Ondas
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollan un marco teórico utilizando núcleos tangentes neurales para comprender por qué la inversión de forma de onda completa de representación continua utilizando redes neuronales alivia la dependencia de la precisión del modelo inicial. El análisis revela una decadencia de valores propios no constante en el NTK basado en ondas, explicando tanto las ventajas de robustez del método como la convergencia más lenta de alta frecuencia. Proponen variantes mejoradas, incluido un enfoque híbrido que combina representaciones neuronales con redes multirresolución, demostrando un rendimiento superior en puntos de referencia de exploración geofísica.

title: Early Discoveries of Algorithmist I: Promise of Provable Algorithm Synthesis at Scale
summary: Researchers present Algorithmist, an autonomous AI agent system that uses multi-agent LLM collaboration to synthesize provably correct algorithms tailored to specific constraints and datasets. The system demonstrated the ability to design algorithms meeting privacy, approximation, and interpretability requirements while discovering improved algorithms in certain settings. This work points toward a paradigm where AI systems generate research-quality algorithmic artifacts customized to individual deployment scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.22363v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Designing algorithms with provable guarantees that also work well in practice remains difficult, requiring both mathematical reasoning and careful implementation. Existing approaches that bridge worst-case theory and empirical performance, such as beyond-worst-case analysis and data-driven algorithm selection, typically assume prior distributional knowledge or restrict attention to a fixed pool of algorithms. Recent progress in LLMs suggests a new possibility: provable algorithm synthesis on the fly. To study this, we built Algorithmist, an autonomous researcher agent on top of GitHub Copilot that runs a multi-agent research-and-review loop, with separate stages for idea generation, algorithm and proof development, proof-guided implementation, and review of proofs, code, and their alignment. We evaluate Algorithmist on research-level tasks in private data analysis and clustering. When asked to design practical methods that jointly satisfy privacy, approximation, and interpretability requirements, it produced provably sound and empirically effective algorithms, together with research-style writeups and audited implementations. It also found improved algorithms in some settings, explained principled barriers in others, and uncovered a subtle proof bug in prior published work. More broadly, our results suggest a new paradigm in which LLM systems generate research-paper-quality algorithmic artifacts tailored to each dataset and deployment setting. They also point to a proof-first code-synthesis paradigm, in which code is developed alongside a structured natural-language proof intermediate representation and kept aligned with it throughout synthesis.

zh.title: Algorithmist的早期发现：大规模可证明算法综合的承诺
zh.summary: 研究人员推出了Algorithmist，一个自主AI代理系统，利用多代理大语言模型协作来综合符合特定约束和数据集的可证明正确的算法。该系统展示了设计满足隐私、近似和可解释性要求的算法的能力，同时在某些设置中发现改进的算法。这项工作指向一个范式，其中AI系统生成研究质量的算法工件，针对不同的部署场景进行定制。

fr.title: Découvertes Précoces d'Algorithmist I : Promesse de la Synthèse d'Algorithmes Prouvables à Grande Échelle
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent Algorithmist, un système d'agent IA autonome qui utilise la collaboration multi-agents LLM pour synthétiser des algorithmes provablement corrects adaptés à des contraintes et des ensembles de données spécifiques. Le système a démontré la capacité à concevoir des algorithmes répondant aux exigences de confidentialité, d'approximation et d'interprétabilité tout en découvrant des algorithmes améliorés dans certains contextes. Ce travail pointe vers un paradigme où les systèmes IA génèrent des artefacts algorithmiques de qualité recherche personnalisés pour des scénarios de déploiement individuels.

de.title: Frühe Entdeckungen des Algorithmist I: Versprechen der beweisbaren Algorithmen-Synthese im großen Maßstab
de.summary: Forscher präsentieren Algorithmist, ein autonomes KI-Agenten-System, das Multi-Agent-LLM-Zusammenarbeit nutzt, um nachweislich korrekte Algorithmen zu synthetisieren, die auf spezifische Einschränkungen und Datensätze zugeschnitten sind. Das System demonstrierte die Fähigkeit, Algorithmen zu entwerfen, die Datenschutz-, Approximations- und Interpretierbarkeitsanforderungen erfüllen, während es in bestimmten Einstellungen verbesserte Algorithmen entdeckt. Diese Arbeit weist auf ein Paradigma hin, in dem KI-Systeme Forschungsqualität-algorithmische Artefakte generieren, die auf individuelle Bereitstellungsszenarien zugeschnitten sind.

es.title: Descubrimientos Tempranos de Algorithmist I: Promesa de Síntesis de Algoritmos Demostrables a Escala
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan Algorithmist, un sistema de agente de IA autónomo que utiliza colaboración multi-agente LLM para sintetizar algoritmos demostrablemente correctos adaptados a restricciones y conjuntos de datos específicos. El sistema demostró la capacidad de diseñar algoritmos que cumplan con requisitos de privacidad, aproximación e interpretabilidad mientras descubrían algoritmos mejorados en ciertos escenarios. Este trabajo señala un paradigma donde los sistemas de IA generan artefactos algorítmicos de calidad de investigación personalizados para escenarios de implementación individuales.

title: MCLR: Improving Conditional Modeling in Visual Generative Models via Inter-Class Likelihood-Ratio Maximization and Establishing the Equivalence between Classifier-Free Guidance and Alignment Objectives
summary: Researchers propose MCLR, a training objective for diffusion models that explicitly maximizes inter-class likelihood ratios to improve conditional image generation without requiring inference-time classifier-free guidance. The work establishes theoretical equivalence between CFG-guided sampling and their alignment-based training objective, providing mechanistic insights into guidance mechanisms. Models fine-tuned with MCLR achieve CFG-comparable performance using standard reverse-time sampling, reducing computational overhead at inference.
content:
arXiv:2603.22364v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Diffusion models have achieved state-of-the-art performance in generative modeling, but their success often relies heavily on classifier-free guidance (CFG), an inference-time heuristic that modifies the sampling trajectory. From a theoretical perspective, diffusion models trained with standard denoising score matching (DSM) are expected to recover the target data distribution, raising the question of why inference-time guidance is necessary in practice. In this work, we ask whether the DSM training objective can be modified in a principled manner such that standard reverse-time sampling, without inference-time guidance, yields effects comparable to CFG. We identify insufficient inter-class separation as a key limitation of standard diffusion models. To address this, we propose MCLR, a principled alignment objective that explicitly maximizes inter-class likelihood-ratios during training. Models fine-tuned with MCLR exhibit CFG-like improvements under standard sampling, achieving comparable qualitative and quantitative gains without requiring inference-time guidance. Beyond empirical benefits, we provide a theoretical result showing that the CFG-guided score is exactly the optimal solution to a weighted MCLR objective. This establishes a formal equivalence between classifier-free guidance and alignment-based objectives, offering a mechanistic interpretation of CFG.

zh.title: MCLR: 通过类间似然比最大化改进视觉生成模型中的条件建模并建立分类器自由指导与对齐目标之间的等价性
zh.summary: 研究人员提出MCLR，一个用于扩散模型的训练目标，显式最大化类间似然比以改进条件图像生成，而无需推理时的分类器自由指导。该工作建立了CFG引导采样与其基于对齐的训练目标之间的理论等价性，提供了对指导机制的机制洞察。使用MCLR微调的模型用标准反向时间采样实现CFG可比性能，减少推理时的计算开销。

fr.title: MCLR: Améliorer la Modélisation Conditionnelle dans les Modèles Génératifs Visuels via la Maximisation du Ratio de Vraisemblance Interclasses et l'Établissement de l'Équivalence entre les Objectifs de Guidance sans Classificateur et d'Alignement
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent MCLR, un objectif d'entraînement pour les modèles de diffusion qui maximise explicitement les ratios de vraisemblance interclasses pour améliorer la génération d'images conditionnelles sans nécessiter une guidance sans classificateur au moment de l'inférence. Le travail établit l'équivalence théorique entre l'échantillonnage guidé par CFG et son objectif d'entraînement basé sur l'alignement, fournissant des perspectives mécanistes sur les mécanismes de guidance. Les modèles affinés avec MCLR atteignent des performances comparables à CFG en utilisant un échantillonnage standard en temps inverse, réduisant les frais de calcul à l'inférence.

de.title: MCLR: Verbesserung der bedingten Modellierung in visuellen generativen Modellen durch Maximierung des Klassenzwischen-Likelihood-Ratio und Etablierung der Äquivalenz zwischen klassifikatorfrei geführten und Ausrichtungszielen
de.summary: Forscher schlagen MCLR vor, ein Trainingsziel für Diffusionsmodelle, das explizit Klassenzwischen-Likelihood-Ratios maximiert, um die bedingte Bildgenerierung ohne erforderliche Inferenz-Zeit-klassifikatorfrei geführte Anleitung zu verbessern. Die Arbeit etabliert die theoretische Äquivalenz zwischen CFG-geführter Abtastung und ihrem ausrichtungsgestützten Trainingsziel und bietet mechanistische Einblicke in Anleitungsmechanismen. Mit MCLR abgestimmte Modelle erreichen CFG-vergleichbare Leistung unter Verwendung von standardmäßiger Rückwärtszeitabtastung und reduzieren den Rechneraufwand bei der Inferenz.

es.title: MCLR: Mejora de la Modelización Condicional en Modelos Generativos Visuales mediante Maximización de Ratios de Probabilidad Interclase y Establecimiento de Equivalencia entre Objetivos de Orientación Libre de Clasificador y Alineación
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen MCLR, un objetivo de entrenamiento para modelos de difusión que maximiza explícitamente los ratios de probabilidad entre clases para mejorar la generación de imágenes condicionales sin requerir orientación libre de clasificador en el momento de la inferencia. El trabajo establece la equivalencia teórica entre el muestreo guiado por CFG y su objetivo de entrenamiento basado en alineación, proporcionando información mecanística sobre los mecanismos de orientación. Los modelos ajustados con MCLR logran un rendimiento comparable a CFG utilizando muestreo de tiempo inverso estándar, reduciendo la sobrecarga computacional en la inferencia.

title: Q-AGNN: Quantum-Enhanced Attentive Graph Neural Network for Intrusion Detection
